sourcePrompt: A coopered wooden barrel standing upright, about ninety centimeters tall. Vertical oak staves swell to a proud belly at the middle and taper to flat round ends, bound by four dark iron hoops - two near the ends, two flanking the bulge - with a small bung hole in one stave. Weathered brown oak and rust-spotted iron, sturdy and timeless. Low-poly, game-ready, clean hard edges.
